name: Source Fidelity appraisal for Tad Safety Interactions Adverse Events bundle
summary: Accepted. The staged source metadata, locators, label date, PMID/DOI fields, population/exposure descriptions, and extracted numeric safety statements match the accessible source records used for this baseline. DailyMed confirms the CIALIS label was updated March 3, 2026 and revised 3/2026; PubMed/NCBI metadata support the clinical pharmacology, pooled safety, and BPH meta-analysis records. Several non-label sources remain abstract-only extractions, which is recorded as a non-blocking source-depth limitation.

findings.claim_or_issue: The DailyMed CIALIS source is current to the staged March 2026 label revision and supports the contraindication, warning, dosing, interaction, and adverse-event sections used in the extraction.
findings.why_it_matters: Safety-boundary claims should be anchored to current labeling before benefit-oriented off-label evidence is interpreted.
findings.recommended_action: No correction required; keep the label revision date visible in future safety updates.

findings.claim_or_issue: The nitrate, alpha-blocker, pooled antihypertensive, and BPH meta-analysis extractions rely primarily on abstracts or summary pages for this baseline, even where deeper full text may be available.
findings.why_it_matters: Abstract-only extraction is adequate for a first safety boundary but can miss eligibility criteria, event definitions, dose subgroup detail, and conflicts or protocol limits.
findings.recommended_action: Keep source-depth caveats in the claim and prioritize full-text extraction before increasing certainty or deriving finer-grained absolute-risk estimates.
